title: Wild Oasis Application Planning

learningObjectives.text: Explain architecture planning using a new example and justify the result.
Apply feature decomposition using a new example and justify the result.
Explain technology choices using a new example and justify the result.

whyThisMatters: The ideas in wild oasis application planning recur throughout later React work, reducing memorization and debugging later. It belongs to the **Professional React Workflows and Ecosystem** module.
mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Architecture planning** → **Feature decomposition** → **Technology choices**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

sections.title: Architecture planning
sections.body: Architecture planning identifies data, responsibilities, boundaries, and communication paths. The goal is to reduce costly ambiguity about ownership and change, not predict every future file.

sections.title: Feature decomposition
sections.body: Start with user-visible outcomes and convert them into explicit behavior, inputs, edge cases, and acceptance conditions. Break large capabilities into end-to-end slices that can be implemented and verified independently.

sections.title: Technology choices
sections.body: Architecture planning identifies data, responsibilities, boundaries, and communication paths. The goal is to reduce costly ambiguity about ownership and change, not predict every future file.

commonMistakes: Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.
Using global state before identifying the real owner and consumers.
Optimizing renders without a measured user-facing problem.
summary: Wild Oasis Application Planning connects Architecture planning, Feature decomposition, Technology choices.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

questions.prompt: A team is making a decision involving Wild Oasis Application Planning. Which approach shows the strongest understanding of **Architecture planning**?

choices.label: State the requirement and assumptions, apply the Architecture planning model, and verify the observable consequence before adding complexity.
choices.feedback: Transferable understanding of Architecture planning means using the model to make and verify a decision in a new context.

choices.label: Use the term Architecture planning in documentation without changing any decision or behavior.

questions.prompt: Which statement best distinguishes **Architecture planning** from **Feature decomposition** in this topic?

choices.label: They address different parts of the topic's model; a correct decision identifies which one explains the current requirement and how the two interact.
choices.feedback: The concepts are related but should be applied according to the requirement and the role each plays in Wild Oasis Application Planning.

choices.label: They are interchangeable labels, so choosing between them has no effect on reasoning.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: The concepts are related but should be applied according to the requirement and the role each plays in Wild Oasis Application Planning.
questions.hint: Look for a distinction based on responsibilities or consequences, not an absolute rule.

questions.prompt: A developer notices this recurring mistake: “Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.” What is the best response?

choices.label: Return to the requirement, use the Wild Oasis Application Planning model to identify the failed assumption, and verify the corrected behavior with a representative case.
choices.feedback: The useful response corrects both the misconception and the next development action for Wild Oasis Application Planning.

choices.label: Hide the symptom and continue so the schedule is not affected.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Add unrelated abstractions before reproducing the problem.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Treat the mistake as unavoidable and remove the relevant check.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: The useful response corrects both the misconception and the next development action for Wild Oasis Application Planning.
questions.hint: Choose the response that makes the failure reproducible and the correction verifiable.

questions.prompt: Which day-to-day workflow is most reliable when a task depends on Wild Oasis Application Planning?

choices.label: Clarify the requirement, identify the relevant model or boundary, test the smallest representative case, then refactor after the behavior is understood.
choices.feedback: A reliable workflow makes assumptions and failures visible early, then improves the implementation after correctness is established.

choices.label: Start with the largest available abstraction and postpone defining expected behavior.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Copy a solution from another project without checking whether its assumptions match.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Optimize for line count first and inspect correctness only after release.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: A reliable workflow makes assumptions and failures visible early, then improves the implementation after correctness is established.
questions.hint: Prefer the sequence that produces evidence early and keeps decisions reversible.

questions.prompt: Which guideline shows the best engineering judgment about Wild Oasis Application Planning?

choices.label: Use Wild Oasis Application Planning to reduce uncertainty: document the requirement, choose a proportionate approach, and leave evidence that another developer can review.
choices.feedback: Use Wild Oasis Application Planning to reduce uncertainty: document the requirement, choose a proportionate approach, and leave evidence that another developer can review.

choices.label: Use Wild Oasis Application Planning in every file because more abstraction always improves software.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Avoid Wild Oasis Application Planning completely because every additional concept reduces maintainability.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

choices.label: Choose only by the number of lines and ignore correctness, ownership, and future change.
choices.feedback: This choice does not resolve the assessed issue. Re-trace the Wild Oasis Application Planning rule and the observable result.

questions.explanation: Use Wild Oasis Application Planning to reduce uncertainty: document the requirement, choose a proportionate approach, and leave evidence that another developer can review.
questions.hint: Choose the option that balances correctness, clarity, cost, and the actual requirement.

researchSources.usage: Coverage research only; no transcript wording is canonical.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.

assessmentProfile.label: Developer workflow and decision scenarios
assessmentProfile.rationale: The learner should make realistic development decisions using requirements, logs, file structures, configuration, or short code artifacts.
assessmentProfile.inLessonPolicy: Use concise concept checks; coding and hybrid topics include at least one code-reading check.
assessmentProfile.topicQuizPolicy: Questions Q04–Q08 form the topic quiz. The presentation mix follows this profile.
